content:
4 min read
Just now

--

How ditching subscriptions and traditional payment processors became the best decision I made for my indie dev tool, CodeGraphic.
You just finished writing some code. Maybe it’s a really good React hook. You are proud of it. You want to show your friends.
So you take a screenshot. But it looks bad. The text is blurry. The font looks ugly. There’s a taskbar in the corner. Your great code now looks messy and boring.
That is the problem I wanted to fix. So I built CodeGraphic.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

But building the tool was only half the job. The other half was figuring out how to sell it. That part was harder than I thought. It changed how I run my whole project.
The Problem With Other Screenshot Tools
Screenshot tools already exist. You have probably used one before. But most of them have problems.
Some are too big and slow. They have too many buttons and menus. A simple task takes way too long.
Others are too plain. They just add a boring background behind your code. It still looks cheap.
I wanted something different. I wanted a tool that felt clean and simple, like a small design studio. Something that opens fast and just works.
To build it, I used three tools: Vite, React, and TypeScript. These help CodeGraphic load fast and feel smooth.
The goal was simple. You paste your code in. You get a clean, pretty picture out. That’s it. No extra steps.
That part was fun to build.
Then I Hit a Big Problem
Once the tool worked, I had a new question. How do people pay for it?
This sounds easy. It is not.
Big payment companies are not made for small builders like me. They are made for big businesses with teams of people. If I sell too much, too fast, they might freeze my account. Then I would need to fight with customer support for weeks.
I also don’t like subscriptions. I already pay for too many apps every month. I did not want to do that to my users too. Nobody wants a surprise charge on their bank statement three months later.
So I asked myself a new question. What if I skip banks and subscriptions completely?
My Fix: Skip the Bank
Instead of using a normal payment company, I used something called NOWPayments. It lets people pay me directly. No bank in the middle. No waiting days for approval. No scary emails saying my account is “under review.”
This changed everything. It let me build a better way to charge for the tool.
CodeGraphic does not use subscriptions. Instead, you buy credits one time. Here is how it works:

• 5 free exports. No card needed. Just try it first.

• $9 for 30 credits. Good if you only use it sometimes.

• $19 for 50 credits. Good for regular use.

• $39 for Lifetime Access. Pay once. Use it forever. No credits to count.
You only pay once for each option. Nothing repeats. Nothing bills you later without asking.
This keeps things fair. It protects your wallet. It also keeps my life simple, since I don’t have a big support team to fight bank problems for me.
Why This Matters to You
You might think this is just a story about me and Stripe. But it actually changes what you get as a user.
Here is why. When a tool uses subscriptions, the company wants you to keep paying, even if you stop using it. But when a tool is pay-once, like mine, I only win if you are happy with it right away. I can’t trick you into paying forever.
You can start with the 5 free exports. If you like it, buy a small credit pack. If you know you’ll use it a lot, get lifetime access. No pressure. No tricks.
If you are tired of tools that quietly take your money every month, this tool is made for you.
What CodeGraphic Actually Does
Let’s talk about the tool itself, not just the payments.

• You paste in your code. You get a clean, sharp picture in seconds.

• The colors and fonts look professional, not random.

• The screen is simple. No clutter. No pop-ups asking you to watch a tutorial.

• You can export pictures sized for Twitter, LinkedIn, blogs, or docs.

• It is fast. You open it, take your picture, and go back to coding.
I am also still building new things. I add new themes and new features often, since it’s just me working on it. If you ask for a new feature, I might add it the same week. There is no long wait list. Your idea goes straight to the person who builds the app: me.
CodeGraphic is not trying to be a giant design app like Figma. It is not trying to do everything. It just wants to do one thing really well: turn your code into a picture you’re proud to share.
Try It Yourself
Do you know that feeling of a blurry, messy code screenshot? Are you tired of apps that quietly charge your card every month? I built CodeGraphic to fix both problems.
Check out CodeGraphic here
Start with 5 free exports. No card needed. If you like it, grab a credit pack or go lifetime. Pay once, or nothing at all to start, and get back to writing code that looks as good as it works.
